title: Nick Jonas' New Album Cover for 'Sunday Best' Honors His Diabetes Diagnosis
summary: Nick Jonas talks to TODAY.com about his new album "Sunday Best," how he honors his diabetes diagnosis and which song is dedicated to his daughter.
story: For over 20 years, Nick Jonas has drawn inspiration from his life for his music, including his Type 1 diabetes diagnosis.

The singer and one-third of the Jonas Brothers was diagnosed with the chronic condition at 13. To express his feelings about his diagnosis, he put pen to paper, resulting in the emotional and reflective song “A Little Bit Longer” — included in the Jonas Brothers’ 2008 album of the same name.
Now age 33, Jonas has again been inspired by his health journey, highlighting his continuous glucose monitor (CGM) on the cover of his new album, “Sunday Best.”

“This album for me was all about transparency. I worked with some incredible co-writers who really helped me articulate some things that I’ve been thinking and feeling for a long time,” Jonas tells TODAY.com. “The fact that the music is some of my most transparent and vulnerable that I’ve ever written, I wanted the album cover to symbolize, first of all, this idea of looking at yourself in the mirror.”
Jonas says there are moments when he and others ask themselves: “Are we being the best version of ourselves today? Are we being nice to ourselves today?”

“And that’s why I wanted that reflective moment,” he explains. “When I looked at the frame and saw the Dexcom G7 on my arm, I was really excited at the idea of leaning in and not doing a version without it because I want people to see this is the real me. This is what my life is like, day in, day out, living with Type 1 diabetes.”

Type 1 diabetes occurs when the pancreas doesn't make enough insulin, causing glucose to build up in the bloodstream, which can lead to a range of complications, according to the American Diabetes Association. The condition is usually diagnosed in kids but can develop at any time, and doctors don't know why. There's no cure, and the primary treatment is taking insulin.
“(Diabetes) is a part of my story. It’s part of my DNA at this point,” Jonas says.

“I’m really going to go to very honest places with this (new) music and (speaking) about a number of things ... my relationship, fatherhood, my experiences, and living with Type 1 diabetes for over 20 years is a huge part of my story," he says.
Below, Jonas dives deeper into how he manages his Type 1 diabetes, the most personal songs on “Sunday Best,” and what he’s most looking forward to in 2026.
This interview has been edited and condensed for clarity.
How Do You Manage Your Diabetes Day to Day?

People living with Type 1 diabetes have to make hundreds of decisions a day to better manage their disease, little things like when you need a snack, or adjusting your insulin delivery, just watching the trends of your glucose. These are all things that add extra layers to an already very busy life and hectic life.

What I try to do is make that next best decision and then, obviously, staying active and getting your body moving is always really helpful. When you’re on tour, it’s tough to find time sometimes, so even just a nice, long walk can do.

I think that there's a growing conversation around the mental and emotional health aspects of living with diabetes, and things like diabetes burnout. It's been great to see that awareness continue to grow and to be a small part of that conversation.
Which Songs on Your New Album, 'Sunday Best,' Reference Your Health Journey?

The first song of the album is called "Sweet to Me," and it is about a lot of different things, but the first verse begins talking about the safety and innocence of childhood — memories like going to Blockbuster with my dad and the hot pizza box on my lap, and how visceral that feeling of just knowing that you're safe to walk around your neighborhood and everyone knows each other's names, and wanting to get back in touch with those simpler days.
As the song progresses, the second verse touches on a lyric, which I love: "When the doctor told me there's too much sugar in your bloodstream, baby, that's too sweet. It's just too sweet."

When I wrote "A Little Bit Longer," it was this more negative and harder to comprehend side of living with Type 1. But now I have a real appreciation for it and the experiences I've had in my work with Beyond Type 1, my nonprofit, but also meeting people in this community and being able to use my platform as a way to speak about something that's incredibly important to me.

It has given me a lot of wonderful, life-changing memories, and it's really nice to see that contrast in my songwriting, too. I have a wonderful wife and a 4-year-old daughter, and I just see everything through the lens of how grateful I am to have my family by my side and still be doing what I’m doing. It’s not to say that there aren’t tough days, but I see everything through that lens of how the song says, “It’s just so sweet to me.”
With the Single 'Gut Punch,' Why Was It Important for You to Be So Vulnerable?
It’s probably easy for some people to look at my life and think that I never have thoughts like that, but that’s just fundamentally not true. The themes in this album are universal.

You don’t have to have lived my life to understand what it’s like to look at yourself in the mirror and question yourself, (have) self-doubt, negative self-talk. I’ve loved hearing from people about how the song has been healing for them and seeing online people connecting to the bridge. It’s about getting back in touch with your inner child.
How Do You Manage Your Diabetes on Tour?

Having access to the frequency of the glucose readings that I get really helps. I saw a major change the minute I got on CGM. As the technology has gotten better, the ability to integrate into the smartphone has been really helpful.

In addition, it's about just trying my best each day to handle it with as much care as I can. Also knowing that there are going to be some days that are tough and unpredictable, and to not get stressed about that and to be upfront with the people you love in your inner circle about, "Hey, my glucose is high or it's low." Not even saying, I need you to do anything, but just to alert them so that they're aware in case something comes up.
What Are Your Go-To Snacks for Managing Your Blood Sugar?

If it’s low, Welch’s Fruit Snacks are always good. Generally, I eat a pretty low-carb diet, some good protein, macronutrients and then a sustaining carb, something that helps if I’m going on the stage, a good baseline. But also it’s difficult when you’re on the road, there’s not a lot of choices. Sometimes you just have to improvise and figure it out.
What Are You Looking Forward to in 2026?

I join my “Jumanji” cast mates again pretty soon, which will be fun, getting back on the “Jumanji” set. There’s another film project, which is coming together right now and aiming to start production fairly soon, which would be great because it’s a project I’ve been developing for a while, and the development process can be very long and exhausting, so when you finally get to a start date, it's very exciting. It's top secret, but there'll be an announcement about it soon. And a lot of shows, both with the brothers and solo throughout the rest of the year.

title: Why Robert Irwin Is Rooting for the Seahawks in the Super Bowl
summary: Robert Irwin says he's rooting for the Seattle Seahawks in Super Bowl 60 because of his mother's ties to the region.
story: It somehow seems fitting that Robert Irwin would be rooting for a hawk — a Seahawk, that is.
The conservationist, who is from Australia, says he is pulling for the Seattle Seahawks to beat the New England Patriots in Super Bowl 60.
“I don’t think people realize just how huge the NFL is in Australia,” he told TODAY.com at the NFL Honors in San Francisco on Feb. 6.
“I grew up watching the Super Bowl because my mom is from Oregon, Pacific Northwest. So I got to back Seattle. I love my Pacific Northwesters.”
The "Dancing with the Stars" champ also has an affinity for the Seahawks because their punter, Michael Dickson, is from Down Under.
“They’ve also got an Australian in their midst. The punter, he’s in there. So I think they’re going to take it out this year,” he said.

Irwin is further psyched that the NFL will continue to expand its schedule outside of the United States by holding its first-ever game in Australia next season when the San Francisco 49ers and Los Angeles Rams play in Melbourne.
No date has yet been finalized, but Irwin is all in on how the Super Bowl unites people.
“I love the vibe. I love the energy. I love the community because just walking around, I keep running into people that I know,” he said.
“It’s amazing how many different people it brings together. And we’ve got a game coming to Australia this year, so I’m proud to represent a little bit of Australia on the carpet tonight.”
Super Bowl 60 takes place Feb. 8 at 6:30. The game will be broadcast live on NBC and streamed on Peacock.

title: Meet Patriots Quarterback Drake Maye's Wife, Ann Michael Maye
summary: Who is Drake Maye's wife, Ann Michael Maye? Here's what to know about Ann Michael Maye including her career as a food TikToker, her NBC show, her athletic career, her age and more.
story: From high school football games to Super Bowl LX, Patriots quarterback Drake Maye has had the same girl cheering on the sidelines for him.
The QB met his wife, Ann Michael Maye, formerly Ann Michael Hudson, all the way back in middle school.

Going into nearly two years of marriage, Drake Maye, 23, has an opportunity to claim another ring this year with hopes of winning this year’s Super Bowl, where the Patriots are set to face off against the Seattle Seahawks.

“Her watching my middle school football games playing quarterback to now getting a chance to watch me in the super bowl … I wouldn’t rather do it with anybody else,” Drake Maye said in a press conference.
At the big match, Ann Michael Maye, 22, will be cheering Drake Maye on.

"WE ARE GOING TO THE SUPER BOWL!!!!!" Ann Michael Maye wrote in the caption of an Instagram post shared after the Patriots' AFC Championship win. "Words can’t even begin to describe how proud I am of you, #10!!!! Watching your dreams come true is the biggest blessing and I love you!!!!!"
Here’s what to know about Ann Michael Maye and her own formidable career.
They Met in Middle School
The couple have been linked since middle school, according to a TikTok throwback Ann Michael Maye posted in 2024.
The couple celebrated their nine-year anniversary in 2024.
“9 years with you and it keeps getting better everyday❤️ I couldn’t imagine my life with out you as my best friend. I love you more than you know!” Ann Michael Maye wrote on Instagram.
Both Attended the University of North Carolina
After high school, Drake Maye went on to play football at University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill starting in 2021.
Ann Michael Maye also went to UNC, studying business administration with an entrepreneurship and conflict management double minor, according to her LinkedIn.
She was also the vice president of finance at her sorority, Kappa Delta. She also completed two strategy internships.
The Couple Married in 2025
Drake Maye got down on one knee with an ocean view and announced their engagement on Instagram on January 15, 2025.
“Love doing life with you. Can’t wait to marry you!” he wrote in a caption.
The couple exchanged vows later that year on June 21, according to Ann Michael Maye’s Instagram. The next day, they shared photos from the wedding on Instagram.
“What a night! Cheers to forever🥂I love you!” Drake Maye wrote.
Drake Maye Is Happy He Married Young
During a conference with the media ahead of the Super Bowl, Drake Maye shared the relationship advice he gives his Patriots teammates.

“My teammates have the right to whatever they choose but I would definitely advise them to eventually get married,” he said. “It’s one of the best things I’ve experienced in my life. There’s no better feeling than coming home to a wife that loves you and cares for you.”
“I know my teammates, some of them are experiencing different parts of their life. I chose to get married young, and I don’t regret it one bit,” he continued.
She's an Athlete
Ann Michael Maye played sports in high school, including softball and basketball.
"She's a great athlete," Drake Maye said at a press conference. "She beats me at a lot of sports."
Ann Michael Maye Is Now a Food Influencer
The 22-year-old has a large social media presence. She shares cooking and day-in-the-life content with over 500,000 followers on TikTok.
Leading up to Christmas of last year, she baked something different for 25 days as part of a challenge called Bakemas.

In early 2026, she debuted her NBC Sports Boston baking show, “Beyond Bakemas.” So far, she's been joined by guests like Bartsool’s Dave Portnoy, former Patriot star David Andrews, and content creator Emma Pritchard.
In Episode 2, she disclosed which hungry football players have been especially appreciative of her baking skills.
“The O-line is the main recipient of my baked goods,” she said.

Ann Michael Maye said that the players will send her funny thank you notes for the sweet treats, with messages like, “We need to protect your husband so we need to keep on the extra weight,” and, “If you need a bodyguard, we’ll be your bodyguard.”
The famous cookie that's been fueling the Patriots ahead of Super Bowl weekend is Ann Michael's "Cinnamon Roll Snickerdoodles," which Drake Maye has claimed to be his favorite.
Fans Absolutely Love Her
Drake Maye is not the only one obsessed with his wife, Ann Michael Maye is adored by the Patroit’s fandom. She’s been dubbed “Queen of the North” and “First Lady of New England.”
"I'm only watching the Super Bowl because Ann is watching the Super Bowl," one fan commented on her TikTok.
Fans expressed why they can't get enough of Ann Michael in a TODAY segment that aired Feb. 6.
"I just think she's so adorable and so unapologetically herself," Merrick Moore said.
"It’s kind of nice because she doesn’t make Drake her personality," Madye Moore said.
In preparation for the big game on Sunday, there are even "I ❤️ Ann Michael" sweatshirts being sold.
Ann Michael Maye Cheers Drake Maye On in Style
Ann Michael Maye attends her husband’s games and isn’t shy about her fashion. She typically stunts in comfortable yet colorful and bold attire.
Most recently, she served at the AFC Championship game, wearing a retro collaged Patriot coat with bright red pants.
What She's Packing for the Super Bowl
On Feb. 4, Ann Michael Maye posted a TikTok of what she’s packing to the Super Bowl, and it includes three suitcases.
She added a luggage tag that says, "Go Pats 10," referencing her husband's number.

In her carryon, she had needlepoint, a "Harry Potter" book, a travel-sized mahjong set, a devotion book and her journal and of course a Patriots Hat. On the way, she finished a needlepoint luggage tag and also made one for her husband that says, "I love Drake Maye."
It's Ann Michael, Not Ann
Drake Maye pointed out that his wife's full first name is Ann Michael, not Ann.
"Her name's Ann Michael, not just Ann. I don't like people calling her Ann and she doesn't like it either," he said in a press conference.

title: ‘The Traitors’ Season 4, Episode 8 Recap: Who Was Banished and Who Was Murdered?
summary: In Episode 8, viewers learned if the Traitors were able to temporarily put aside their differences and continue working together. Read on for a recap of Episode 8, below.
story: Warning: Spoilers for Season 4, Episode 8 of “The Traitors” below…
As the end of the game draws closer, the Season 4 Traitors are struggling to keep their identities a secret.

Last week on “The Traitors,” “The Real Housewives of Beverly Hills” alum and original Traitor Lisa Rinna was banished by the group partly due to “Love Island USA” Season 6 star and fellow Traitor Rob Rausch turning on her.

Then, “The Real Housewives of Potomac” star Candiace Dillard Bassett, the third Traitor, countered by casting a vote for Rausch at the Round Table. The episode ended with Rausch and Dillard Bassett meeting in the turret. The two were clearly no longer united in their mission to deceive the Faithfuls, with Rausch’s treacherous move shattering the trust between him and Dillard Bassett.
In Episode 8, which was released on Peacock Thursday, Feb. 5, viewers learned if the Traitors were able to temporarily put aside their differences and continue working together.
So, did they evade suspicion after committing the next murder? Or, did the Faithfuls successfully banish another Traitor? Read on for a recap of Episode 8, below.
Candiace and Rob Go Head-to-Head in the Turret
The episode opens with Rausch attempting to explain to Dillard Bassett why he turned on Rinna.
“As a person, I feel bad. But as a player, Lisa had to go,” he says.

Dillard Bassett shares in her confessional that Rausch is a threat to her, but she has to appear unbothered or else he will stab her in the back. So, she attempts to move past Rinna’s banishment and also tells Rausch that her burn vote against him was meaningless.

Host Alan Cumming then enters the turret and informs the Traitors that they can either commit a murder or recruit a Faithful to their team. Rausch and Dillard Bassett agree that they want to remain a duo before discussing who is on the chopping block. But Dillard Bassett is secretly plotting her next move.
“I need to make Rob feel like he is still a part of a team…a careful assassin serves her revenge cold,” she says in her confessional.
A Calculated Murder

At breakfast, the players who arrive first chat about Rinna’s banishment. Maura Higgins, from the UK version of “Love Island,” notes that it was odd Dillard Bassett chose to vote for Rausch despite an overwhelming majority choosing to banish Rinna. After all the remaining players enter the breakfast room, viewers learn that Dillard Bassett and Rausch chose to murder “The Bachelor” star Colton Underwood.

We flash back to the turret where Dillard Bassett is the one to suggest murdering Underwood. In his final confessional, the former “Bachelor” lead says, “Well that’s a bummer. Damn. I think it has become pretty obvious that Candiace is up there. Trying to be consistent and then do a throw away vote is sloppy gameplay.”
He ironically adds, “I have faith that the Faithfuls know who it is and I hope that my boy Rob wins.”

Dillard Bassett explains to viewers that she wanted to murder Underwood to weaken Rausch. However, the “Love Island USA” star is a step ahead of her. He paraphrases a Mark Twain quote and says, “It ain’t the things you don’t know that hurt you. It’s the things you think you know for sure,” before pointing out that Underwood had mentioned he was suspicious of Dillard Bassett before he was murdered. He withheld this information from Dillard Bassett.
A Picture-Perfect Mission

For the mission, the players have to split themselves into two teams. The team with eight players will be tasked with re-creating portraits based on descriptions written on plaques in the castle. A photo of their re-creation will be sent to an old gallery where the three remaining players will have to sort through a room of paintings and figure out which one matches the picture.

“Dancing With the Stars” pro Mark Ballas, “Top Chef” host Kristen Kish and Rausch are the three who go to the old gallery, which means they also have an opportunity to earn a shield. The group adds $12,000 to the prize pot and Kish finds the shield.
Candiace Calls Out Rob

Back at the castle, Dillard Bassett and “One Tree Hill” star Stephen Colletti’s names are being thrown out. Colletti tells Dillard Bassett about Underwood thinking she was a Traitor before he was murdered, which tips her off that Rausch set her up.

Rausch interrupts Dillard Bassett and Colletti’s conversation and asks to speak to her privately. When she asks him if he has been mentioning her name around the castle, he lies. She then tells him she thinks they have “different values.”
“I feel like you’re taking this very personally,” he replies before reminding her they are playing a game.
Dillard Bassett retorts, “It is a game — and you are a snake.” She warns him that if she gets banished then she will sabotage him on her way out.
Traitors Battle at the Round Table

Olympic figure skater Johnny Weir opens up the discussion at the Round Table by listing the reasons why he suspects Colletti is a Traitor. “The Real Housewives of New York City” alum Dorinda Medley chimes in to add some evidence she believes also points to Colletti being a Traitor. Then, singer Eric Nam mentions Dillard Bassett’s vote the previous week. She reacts by calling it a throwaway vote, but then says she now wonders if Rausch is a Traitor. She recalls him being nervous at breakfast. She also says Rausch was using Underwood to get Rinna out of the game.

After a brief, calm back-and-forth between Dillard Bassett and Rausch, it is time to vote. All the players, except Dillard Bassett and “Survivor” winner Natalie Anderson (who votes for Olympic figure skater Tara Lipinski), vote to banish “The Real Housewives of Potomac” alum.

While standing on the Circle of Truth, Dillard Bassett holds back tears as she says, “I don’t want to cry. I love you all so much. This experience has been life changing. I’ve never been in a situation where I’ve connected with so many people so quickly. I feel like I’ve played this game with valor, with love, with respect… And with treachery because I am a Traitor!”
During her final confessional, Dillard Bassett is in good spirits.

“I am proud of how I played this game. It was really challenging to have to lie. I really started to love everybody. And that’s what just I think stings the most,” she says. “But I’m confident that they’re going to look at Rob. And in the event that they don’t, joke is on them. So good luck to you all.”
A New Traitor Is Recruited

As the last Traitor in the game, Rausch realizes that the other players might start to suspect him and use Dillard Bassett’s previous vote as evidence. He enters the turret alone and Cumming tells him that “per the rules” he must give one Faithful an ultimatum: join him as a Traitor or die.
Rausch goes through the pros and cons of a few players before choosing Nam to be the next Traitor. When Nam receives his recruitment letter, he isn’t pleased.
“I’m almost there. I just want to be left alone,” he says about making it to the end of the game as a Faithful.
Nam leaves the castle and walks to the front steps where he is stunned to see Rausch reveal himself as a Traitor.
In Episode 9, out Feb. 12, viewers will learn if Nam accepts the offer, and if he and Rausch can survive until the end of the game.

title: 'Lincoln Lawyer' Season 4 Ending: Who Killed Sam, Does Mickey Get Out of Jail and More Questions Answered
summary: The ending of Lincoln Lawyer Season 4 explained, including who killed Sam and if Mickey gets out of jail. Find out here.
story: Spoilers ahead for Season 4 of “The Lincoln Lawyer.”
Season 4 of “The Lincoln Lawyer,” which premiered Feb. 4, might just be the heaviest and most emotional yet.

After the Season 3 cliffhanger, which saw Mickey Haller (Manuel Garcia-Rulfo) detained after his former client Sam Scales (Christopher Thornton) was found dead in his trunk, Mickey finds himself behind bars and the prime suspect in the death.

While Mickey heads to court, the rest of his team is overwhelmed with keeping their firm afloat and attempting to solve who is behind Sam's death. Then, there's the sudden and unexpected death of beloved mentor Legal Siegel (Elliott Gould), as well as Mickey and Maggie's (Neve Campbell) daughter Hayley (Krista Warner) being bullied at school.
As the episodes continue, it appears as if the FBI and police department are out to get Mickey, but there’s a bigger scheme at hand.
So what happens at the end of “The Lincoln Lawyer” Season 4 and who really killed Sam? Read on to find out.
What Happens at the End of 'The Lincoln Lawyer' Season 4?
Mickey's ex-wife Maggie, who is a criminal prosecutor, switches to the defense side to help him with his case.
The two are up against Dana “Death Row Dana” Berg (Constance Zimmer), who is one tough prosecutor and thinks Mickey is guilty.
Mickey maintains his innocence and believes he is being framed — and he is, but it's a little tricky.
His team discovers that Sam was involved in a scheme and betrayed another con artist for personal gain.

While investigating, they find that Sam had retrofitted a semi-truck and got a commercial driver’s license under a fake name so he could get a job at a biofuel company that was also running their own scam against the government.

Mickey asks Cisco (Angus Sampson) to track down Alex Gazarian, formerly Alex Grant (Michael A. Goorjian), to be a witness in his case. They believe he might have information on who is trying to frame Mickey.

Alex played a role in Mickey winning lover-turned-client Lisa Trammell's (Lana Parrilla) murder trial in Season 2. After she was accused of murdering real estate developer Mitchell Bondurant, Mickey won his case by making it seem as if construction company owner Alex was behind it.

Alex was then investigated by the FBI for his ties to the Armenian mob. The attention he received from the trial made him angry towards Mickey. In Season 4, a group of Armenian mobsters jump Cisco, which makes Mickey and his team think they might be behind Sam's death and trying to get back at him.

However, as Cisco attempts to subpoena Alex, he is killed by the same men who previously jumped him. Their only chance at finding out what happened to Sam is having Alex's girlfriend, Jeanine Ferrigno (Emmanuelle Chriqui), testify in court.
Jeanine, however, is afraid for her life and refuses to speak in court.
Who Killed Sam and Why?
It turns out that Sam and Alex were working together to defraud the government.

According to Mickey, the only people who could confirm their dealings were FBI Agent Ruth (Sasha Alexander) and Agent Vasquez (Hemky Madera), who previously threatened Mickey at his home. The agents had warned him about sticking his nose where it didn't belong and told him they would never take the stand. But why?
The federal agents are working on an active investigation that involved Alex, Sam and the biofuel companies that are defrauding the government.
The FBI is aware of about a dozen of refineries that are scamming the government by selling the same feedstock or cooking oil over and over again by changing the labels.
“And every time, they bill the government all over again for it,” Agent Ruth tells Mickey. “The players behind the scam are all the same too.”

She tells them that this is where Sam came into the picture. The FBI had hired Sam to be their inside man. Mickey blames the FBI for Sam’s death, suggesting that Alex had found out Sam was working for the feds.
However, that's not the case. Jeanine confesses to Maggie and Cisco that Alex had no idea Sam was working with the FBI.
Alex killed Sam because he found out that Sam was stealing from him.
Alex put Sam in Mickey’s trunk because he was mad at him for making him look guilty when he pleaded the fifth in Lisa’s trial. Because of that trial, Alex lost his construction company.
His family then turned on him because of the missing money and the attention he received from the FBI after the trial.
How Was Mickey Cleared of Sam's Death?
Jeanine refuses to testify but Mickey and his team devise a plan to scare the FBI into thinking that she will speak up and ruin their whole investigation.
While in court, FBI agents show up and tell the judge that Jeanine cannot testify. In exchange for her silence, they are willing to give her protection and offer to drop the charges against Mickey.

Mickey, however, has some conditions before he accepts the dismissed charges. He asks for a public statement from the DA’s office that completely exonerates him of any crime — and he wants Dana to deliver it.
“She is the one calling me a murderer for the last four months. She’s the one the world needs to hear it from,” he says, making a point to get his reputation back and prove he's innocent.
Mickey also asks for there to be an investigation into Officer Roy Collins, who pulled him over and framed him for Sam’s murder.
A Shocking New Discovery
All seems right in Mickey’s world after he is cleared of the murder charges. But in true “Lincoln Lawyer” fashion, something always has to come up.
While he’s at the grocery store, he keeps getting glances from a mystery woman, portrayed by Cobie Smulders. She goes up to him in the parking lot and then tells him to “get down!”
Shots are fired, cars crash and the FBI is at the scene. Agent Ruth appears and tells Mickey they have been following him because they had reason to believe he was still being targeted.
Dead, on the ground, is one of the Armenian mobsters who was about to shoot Mickey before the FBI agents killed him.
Thanks to the mystery woman, he is alive. When he asks who she is, she replies, “I’m your sister.”
And the season ends.
Will There Be a Season 5 of 'The Lincoln Lawyer'?
Yes, ahead of its Season 4 premiere, “The Lincoln Lawyer” was renewed for a fifth season.
According to the show's creator Ted Humphrey and co-showrunner Dailyn Rodriguez, they are “already hard at work on the next one,” per an interview with Tudum published Jan. 29.
“Season 4 is the most challenging and intensely personal journey we’ve taken Mickey Haller on yet, and we’re thrilled and grateful to be able to continue the ride in Season 5!” they added.

title: 1 Man's Quest to Learn to Dougie Is Bringing the Internet Together
summary: JB Star started a daily posting challenge where he tries to learn to Dougie. He's gone viral and gotten a bevy of adoring followers.
story: The year is 2010. Cali Swag District’s “Teach Me How to Dougie” is blasting from radio speakers and into iPods headphones, dominating school dances and house parties alike. Everyone is attempting the signature shoulder-lean move. Some, successfully. Most, not so much.
Sixteen years after the song came out, one man has decided it’s finally time to answer that call. Better late than never, right?
The Dougie, a dance move characterized by its signature shoulder lean and smooth swaying motion, became a cultural phenomenon in the early 2010s.
Jun Bae (JB) Star, a South Korea-based breakdancer who has been documenting his daily attempts to master the Dougie on TikTok and Instagram, has become an unexpected viral sensation.

With each video racking up hundreds of thousands, if not millions, of views, his 42-day (and counting) series proves that watching someone struggle through a decade-old dance move is exactly what the internet needed right now.
“I’ve always wanted to do the Dougie because I didn’t know how,” Star tells TODAY.com. “It was trending back in the 2010s, and I thought it would be a shame if I can’t do the Dougie in my life.”

What started as a modest daily posting challenge in December 2025 quickly transformed into something Star never anticipated. For the first week, his videos received minimal engagement. Then, while traveling internationally for a friend’s wedding, everything changed.
“I was posting at the hotel because it was a daily challenge,” he recalls. “I remember when I was at the wedding, it started blowing up.”
The Day 1 video, an earnest attempt based solely on Star’s mental image of what the Dougie should look like, became social media gold.
“It just looked so… ‘This guy can’t do it at all,’ so it’s kind of funny,” he recalls, laughing. “But that was really just me trying.”

As the days progressed, so did Star’s skills. The videos start with a warmup before Star attempts that day's Dougie. He switches up which version of the song he dances to, sometimes choosing the viral Enya mashup.

Viewers have watched his growth day by day, celebrating small victories and genuine effort as he works toward mastering the dance’s smooth, swaggering essence. The imperfect journey is exactly what has resonated with millions.
The comment sections of the videos have become their own ecosystem, with viewers bonding over their shared investment in JB’s progress.
One comment reads, "Well you may never get the dance down but you make THOUSANDS of people smile everyday so thanks for that!"
"Who do I feel so proud?" another commenter wrote on the Day 39 video.

Others make specific observations about his progress: "Your hips are finally nice and loose and feeling the rhythm! This is the second day in a row I’ve noticed that and I think that’s the biggest win!"
People are invested, to say the least. "I talked to my therapist about your progress this week," another wrote.
Star is well aware of the comments.
“There’s definitely a community that’s built around this series,” Star reflects. “Every morning I wake up, I look at the comments, and that makes me happy because people are engaging genuinely.”
For Star, who has created content for years, the response to this particular series has been unlike anything he’s experienced.

“Some people message me and say, ‘I’m going through a really hard time and this series is something that I look forward to every morning or every day,’” he shares. “Some messages have really impacted me as well. It’s getting bigger than me — the series is more than just me doing these funny videos.”
Star thinks that in a world grappling with political division and the eerie perfection of AI-generated content, his earnest, unfiltered journey offers something rare: genuine human imperfection.
“People see so much negative news or whatever they see that doesn’t necessarily make them feel good,” Star says. “People want to see something more lighthearted, something funny or comedic.”
He also believes the appeal lies in transparency. “What you’re seeing is just me pressing play and recording me learning the dance. It’s simple, straightforward, and easily recognizable,” he says.

Not everyone believes Star’s Dougie journey is authentic. Since the beginning of his social media experiment, skeptics have questioned whether the breakdancer is simply pretending not to know how to Dougie. Some have even accused him of being a member of the Jabbawockeez, the famous masked dance crew.
But Star insists his struggle is genuine. “I’m really not faking it, genuinely just trying my best. I think that’s what resonates with the audience as well,” he says.

The vast majority of his community agrees. Dance creators have offered to teach him via video calls. People from Dallas, Texas — where the Dougie originated — have invited him to learn from the architects themselves.
And perhaps most significantly, a member of Cali Swag District, the group behind the iconic “Teach Me How to Dougie” sensation, reached out to collaborate.

As for when the series will end? Star says he's not sure. As a self-described slow learner who took “years” to become proficient at breakdancing, he expects the Dougie journey to continue for quite a while.
But beyond the technical mastery of a viral dance move, Star has discovered something more profound: the unifying power of art and authentic self-expression.

“There’s so much politics and news that’s dividing people,” he says. “Dance has no borders and everyone can unite just through this simple Dougie dance. I’m learning every day and growing, not just the Dougie dance, but as a human as well through all these interactions.”

title: 'The Lincoln Lawyer' Kills Off a Beloved Character in Season 4. Who Dies and How?
summary: Who dies in Season 4 of The Lincoln Lawyer? Here's what happens to Legal Siegal and how Mickey Haller reacts to his death.
story: Spoilers ahead for Season 4 of “The Lincoln Lawyer.”
“The Lincoln Lawyer” revolves around the deaths and murders that defense attorney Mickey Haller successfully handles. But the latest death in Season 4 of the Netflix legal drama hits home.

The unexpected death comes midseason as Mickey (Manuel Garcia-Rulfo) himself is involved in the murder case involving his former client Sam Scales (Christopher Thornton), who at the end of Season 3 was found in the trunk of his car.

With the stress of Mickey's own legal troubles and future, Lorna (Becki Newton) takes on more cases to help their firm stay afloat and Maggie (Neve Campbell) even steps up by leaving her role as a criminal prosecutor to defend her ex-husband in court.
So it’s a shock when, in the show’s sixth episode of Season 4, a beloved character dies.
Who Dies in Season 4 of 'The Lincoln Lawyer'?
Sadly, David "Legal" Siegel  (Elliott Gould) suffers a heart attack at the end of Episode 6, breaking the hearts of Mickey, Lorna and the rest of the team who loved him.
The episode shows Legal at Mickey's office after he ditches the field trip his assisted living facility planned to the Broad so he could have lunch with the lawyer.
Since Mickey isn't there, Lorna offers to go to lunch with him in hopes that he'll give her advice on a difficult case she's working on.
The two then grab sandwiches — a No. 19 pastrami with Russian dressing for Legal, to be exact — at Langer's Deli.
Lorna and Legal bond over practicing law, with Legal sharing how his children didn't seem interested in his career while Mickey always asked questions and his advice.
Legal has always been a father figure and mentor to Mickey over the years, which Lorna acknowledges. They both express how worried they are about Mickey.
Later on, Legal is seen riding the bus when Lorna calls him to share an update on her case. He congratulates her on a job well done and gives her some words of wisdom.
As Mickey and Maggie deal with his case at the courthouse, Lorna gets a call that leaves her speechless and emotional. When she runs into Mickey and Maggie, she reveals that Legal had a heart attack.
“He was on the bus, and we were talking, I thought everything was fine ... He’s gone, Mickey,” Lorna says. “I’m so sorry. He’s gone.”
Lorna adds that Legal's children told her they will hold a funeral in the next couple of days.
Heartbroken and upset, Mickey returns to his hearing and surrenders himself to the police, waving his speedy trial and going into custody.
Episode 7 begins with Mickey in prison and getting a visit from Legal. However, it’s a dream and Legal jokes that he had “one too many pastrami sandwiches.”
Because he was locked up, Mickey had to miss Legal’s funeral.
“What am I going to do without you, Legal?” Mickey says, with his dear friend replying, “Same thing you did with me. Figure it out.”
Even in the dream scenario, Legal continues to give Mickey advice on his case.

title: 'Minionsgate,' Explained: What Happened with the Olympic Figure Skater Using 'Minions' Music?
summary: Figure skater Tomàs-Llorenç Guarino Sabaté said he has gotten permission to skate his signature "Minions"-themed routine after reporting copyright issues days before the Winter Olympics.
story: Cue the celebratory bananas.

After a potentially disastrous copyright mix-up days before the start of the Winter Games, Spanish figure skater Tomàs-Llorenç Guarino Sabaté said he has obtained permission to skate his signature "Minions"-themed routine, which features music from the "Despicable Me" movies and its spinoff.

“Huge THANK YOU to everyone who reposted, shared, and supported," he said on Instagram Feb. 3. "Because of you, Universal Studios reconsidered and officially granted the rights for this one special occasion."
TODAY.com has reached out to Universal Pictures for comment. (Universal Pictures and TODAY.com share a parent company, NBCUniversal.)
Sabaté has delighted figure skating fans this entire season as he hit the ice dressed as a Minion from the popular film series "Despicable Me."
In the days leading up to the Winter Olympics, however, Sabaté almost had to rework his whimsical routine due to an issue with copyright, in a situation that one viral tweet dubbed "Minionsgate."
Below, we're recapping everything you need to know.
The Figure Skater Has Been Skating a 'Minions'-Themed Program All Season

Sabaté is clearly fond of "Despicable Me." In his Instagram bio, the Spanish figure skater describes himself as a "Minion & Caveman interpret(er)" and he's been sporting a Minions-inspired costume  of denim overalls and a yellow T-shirt while competing his short program throughout the season.

The Minions were first introduced in the 2010 animated comedy, "Despicable Me." They became stars of the franchise, appearing in its two sequels and receiving their own spinoff series — 2015's "Minions" and 2022's "Minions: The Rise of Gru." If you've ever seen the films, you've likely giggled while hearing the Minions sing the iconic Universal Pictures introductory music.
In his recent performances, Sabaté has opened his routine with audio of the Minions singing before transitioning into other songs, with interludes of the Minions speaking.
Days Before the Olympics, the Figure Skater Said He Couldn't Use 'Minions' Music at the Olympics
But on Feb. 2, Sabaté announced that he wasn't allowed to skate to the "Minions" music at the Winter Olympics.

"Hello all, this season I have competed with my Minions routine to bring joy and a fun style to the ice, while complying with all of the obligatory elements and demonstrating that ice skating as a male Olympic figure skater can also be fun," he said in a statement to his Instagram story, per ESPN, People and other outlets.
Sabaté said he followed protocol to get his music approved in August.

"Regrettably, just days before the Olympics begins, I was informed that I am no longer allowed to perform this routine due to copyright issues," he wrote. “Finding this out last Friday, so close to the most important competition of my life, was incredibly disappointing. Nevertheless, I will face this challenge head on and do everything possible to make the best of the situation."
Sabaté Said Universal Granted Him Permission to Use the Music
Sabaté didn't have to wait long to resolve the copyright conundrum. On Feb. 3, he took to Instagram to announce that Universal Studios had granted him permission to skate to the Minions music.

"Huge THANK YOU to everyone who reposted, shared, and supported 💙Because of you, Universal Studios reconsidered and officially granted the rights for this one special occasion," he wrote in an Instagram story.
Carson Daly shared the news on TODAY on Feb. 4 and used a "Despicable Me" reference to cheer on Sabaté.
"Can't wait to see you steal the moon on the ice," he said.

title: "Bridgerton" Author Julia Quinn Recommends 13 Books to Read in 2026
summary: Bridgerton author Julia Quinn recommends 13 books to read in 2026.
story: Season 4 of "Bridgerton" is in full swing and Julia Quinn, the author of the books that inspired the series, stopped by TODAY to share some book recommendations.
Of course, you could always read one of Quinn's books as you wait fro Season 4 to pick up with more episodes (and perhaps find out what happens next.
Otherwise, check out one of Quinn's suggestions.
Best Romance
"And Now, Back to You" by B.K. Borison

What Julia Quinn said: "I’m not saying B.K. Borison has magic powers, but it’s a little suspicious that her romance novel about two meteorologists stranded together during the storm of the century is landing in bookstores right now... as half the country is clearing out from the storm of the century. Seriously though, 'And Now, Back to You' is simply delightful in every way. The characters are charming and real, and the book manages to make you laugh and tug on your heartstrings. Five stars, all the way."
Best Read for Black History Month
"Keeper of Lost Children" by Sadeqa Johnson

What Julia Quinn said: “I love when fiction introduces me to a piece of history I never knew about, and ‘Keeper of Lost Children’ does just that by shining a light on the true story of the ‘Brown Baby Plan,’ a 1950s movement spearheaded by African-American journalist Mabel Grammer to bring biracial children of white German women and Black American GIs to the United States for adoption.’ In Keeper of Lost Children,’ Mabel has been fictionalized as Ethel Gathers, who has moved to Germany with her husband, who is an officer in the US Army. She is shocked when she discovers hundreds of biracial babies living in German orphanages and, after adopting several children herself, begins a one-woman mission to find homes for the rest. I loved everything about this book, from the impeccably researched plot to the beautifully-drawn characters.”
Best Book-to-Screen
"Project Hail Mary" by Andy Weir

What Julia Quinn said: "I loved 'The Martian,' both on page and screen, so it was a no brainer for me to pick this up. Andy Weir puts a lot of science in his science fiction, but he also spins a terrific story with compelling characters, including our protagonist, Ryland Grace, a middle school science teacher who is somehow tasked with saving the world from a climate fueled apocalypse. Fortunately for humanity, Ryland soon teams up with Rocky, an alien life-form who’s a bit like a metallic spider (but with a heart of gold and a keen sense of sarcasm). The stakes in this novel are sky high, but Weir keeps the story grounded with geeky humor, and it’s easy to see why 'Project Hail Mary' is predicted to be one of the biggest blockbuster movies of 2026."
What Julia's Reading
"How to Commit a Postcolonial Murder" by Nina McConigley

What Julia Quinn said: "Every now and then I pick up a book solely because I’m intrigued by the title. What is a postcolonial murder? And why do I want to know how to commit one? Georgie Ayyar Creel is a 12-year-old Indian-American child, who, along with her sister Agatha, are navigating girlhood in a very white Wyoming town where 'Indian' usually means Native American. Their lives are upended by the arrival of their uncle Vinny, who emigrates from India with his family. Vinny molests them, repeatedly, and the two girls decide to poison him slowly with antifreeze. I thought this books would be a mystery, and in some ways it is, but is much more a tale of girlhood, and sisterhood, and all the ways a family bond can fracture. Georgie’s narration is wry and sharp, often speaking directly to the reader, and in this way, we are forced to reckon with her choices as if they were our own. 'How to Commit a Postcolonial Murder' is the kind of book that is difficult to describe, but so very easy to recommend."
More 2026 Recommendations
Quinn also shared a list of books she read and enjoyed for the rest of the year:

"And the Crowd Went Wild" by Susan Elizabeth Phillips (February)"She Fell Away" by Lenore Nash (March)"The Antiquarian’s Object of Desire" by India Holton (April)"Annie Knows Everything" by Rachel Wood (April)"The Paris Match" by Kate Clayborn (April)"Anxietyland" by Gemma Correll (April)"All We Have is Time" by Amy Tordoff (June)"Game of Rogues" by Julie Anne Long (June)"The Missed Connection" by Tia Williams (June)
"And the Crowd Went Wild" by Susan Elizabeth Phillips (February)
"She Fell Away" by Lenore Nash (March)
"The Antiquarian’s Object of Desire" by India Holton (April)
"Annie Knows Everything" by Rachel Wood (April)
"The Paris Match" by Kate Clayborn (April)
"Anxietyland" by Gemma Correll (April)
"All We Have is Time" by Amy Tordoff (June)
"Game of Rogues" by Julie Anne Long (June)
"The Missed Connection" by Tia Williams (June)

title: What Time Does 'Lincoln Lawyer' Season 4 Drop on Netflix?
summary: "The Lincoln Lawyer" returns with Season 4 on February 5, 2026 after the cliffhanger ending in the Netflix show's third season. Find out when the new season streams.
story: Spoilers for Season 3 of "The Lincoln Lawyer."
“The Lincoln Lawyer” is finally back after the shocking cliffhanger at the end of the Netflix show’s third season.
Season 4 of “The Lincoln Lawyer” starring Manuel Garcia-Rulfo as Mickey Haller is based on the sixth book in “The Lincoln Lawyer” series, “The Law of Innocence.”
Mickey is facing his toughest case yet after he was detained at the end of Season 3 when police found Sam Scales’ body in the trunk of his car.

Now, Mickey and his team will work tirelessly to prove his innocence in the murder of his former client and clear his name. According to the season’s synopsis, while Mickey’s team unravels Sam’s final scam, it forces “them to go head-to-head with the DA’s office, the FBI, and ghosts from Mickey’s own past.”
During a Feb. 4 appearance on TODAY, Garcia-Rulfo teased that the new season is the “strongest” one yet, adding, “Or at least the one I enjoyed the most.”
“His life is on the line and he’s on the other side of the table. It just feels heavier and more emotional,” he added about the new episodes.
Read on to learn when “The Lincoln Lawyer” Season 4 will premiere on Netflix in February.
When Does 'The Lincoln Lawyer' Season 4 Premiere?
“The Lincoln Lawyer” Season 4 will premiere on Thursday, Feb. 5.
What Time Will ‘The Lincoln Lawyer’ Season 4 Release?
The fourth season of “The Lincoln Lawyer” will be available on  Feb. 5 at 12:00 a.m. PT/3:00 a.m. ET.
How Many Episodes Will Be In ‘The Lincoln Lawyer’ Season 4?
Season 4 of “The Lincoln Lawyer” will consist of 10 episodes.

title: ‘Traitors’ Host Alan Cumming Reveals His Nickname for Contestant Rob Rausch
summary: “The Traitors” host Alan Cumming is sharing his thoughts on divisive Season 4 star Rob Rausch, including his special nickname for him.
story: “The Traitors” host Alan Cumming is sharing his thoughts on divisive Season 4 star Rob Rausch, including his special nickname for him.

Cumming, who has won an Emmy for his work as the incomparable host of the hit Peacock competition show, appeared on TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle to chat about the current season. Fans have been hooked on all the shenanigans, betrayals, murders and banishments that have occurred so far. The show trends every Thursday when new episodes arrive, and one of the constant main topics of conversation is “Love Island USA” Season 6 alum Rob Rausch.

In Episode 1, Cumming tapped Rausch — along with “The Real Housewives of Beverly Hills” alum Lisa Rinna and former “The Real Housewives of Potomac” star Candiace Dillard Bassett as the three main Traitors. Each episode, Rausch has been reveling in his role as a Traitor and his devious gameplay.
“People are obsessed with it,” Sheinelle said about Season 4 while interviewing Cumming. “Everybody’s talking about how fans are crushing on Rob.”
A picture of Rausch, a snake wrangler from Alabama, then appeared. It showed him in his banquet outfit from the latest episode.
Jenna simply said, “Wow,” when the picture, which highlighted Rausch’s snake tattoos, popped up.
Cumming responded, “I know,” before revealing his nickname for Rausch. “I call him my future ex-boyfriend,” he said as Jenna and Sheinelle laughed.
Later in the interview, Jenna and Sheinelle mentioned that Cumming turned 61 on Jan. 27. They wished him a happy birthday as a photo was shown of the actor with his husband, Grant Shaffer.
Jenna asked, “How does your husband feel about your future ex-boyfriend?”
Cumming jokingly replied, “He’s come to terms because I’ve mentioned it so often.”

Viewers have been enjoying seeing Rausch plotting with Rinna and Dillard Bassett as the trio plan their murders. But, some fans were critical of him last week when he turned on Rinna in Episode 7. At the Round Table, Rausch explained to the Faithfuls why he thought Rinna was a Traitor, betraying one of his own. She was banished at the end of the episode, leaving only Rausch and Dillard Bassett in the turret.
“What was the point of making Rob a traitor if he is going to spend the whole game defending the faithfuls and piling on his fellow traitors….?” one person tweeted after Episode 7 was released.
Others disagreed and supported Rausch’s strategy. Another X user wrote, “Yall can stay mad at rob but he’s the only traitor playing smart sorry.”

Fans will have to wait to see if Rausch’s strategy pays off. They’ll find out if he can mend his strained relationship with Dillard Bassett so the two can continue their mission as Traitors when Episode 8 is released Thursday, Feb. 5.

title: What Is a 'Boy Aquarium' and How Did 'Heated Rivalry' Make It Hockey's Biggest Trend?
summary: What is a boy aquarium? How the viral hockey romance 'Heated Rivalry' made the sport's biggest rebrand go mainstream.
story: It's winter, which means the season of the "boy aquarium" is in full swing. With the advent of "Heated Rivalry" and the promise of Season 2 on the horizon, it’s flourishing more than ever before.
The term refers literally to an ice hockey arena, where groups of friends gather to watch (and admire) the players, a bit like you might do with like fish in a tank.
The idea of “boy aquarium” isn’t new to TikTok, but this year — between the phenomenon that is “Heated Rivalry,” the NHL season and the upcoming Winter Olympics — it’s become impossible to ignore.

People are taking to social media to document their “field trips” to the boy aquarium, aka a trip to an NHL game. According to SeatGeek, there has been a surge in NFL ticket sales attributed to "Heated Rivalry."

The soundtracks of the videos vary, from the moody remix of t.a.T.u’s “All The Things She Said” by DJ Harrison (popularized by "Heated Rivalry"), to more playful choices like the kid’s song “Here Fishie Fishie.”
A Reddit user shared a picture of herself at a hockey arena with a shirt connecting her trip to the "boy aquarium" with "Heated Rivalry."
Some of the captions point to intrigue around the players, with captions like “Can’t a girl just love aquariums?” or “Just what the doctor ordered!”
Others are about the experience itself. A person in a front-row seat at the arena wrote the caption, "The boy aquarium didn't disappoint."
The metaphor also extends to gameplay. While filming a fight between two plays, one TikToker captioned the video, "When someone puts two betas in the same tank at the boy aquarium."
Some creators take it a step further, tapping on the glass to get the players’ attention, mimicking the (strongly discouraged) behavior of aquarium visitors trying to interact with marine life.

Official teams have joined in on the fun, too. The Florida Panthers posted a video to their TikTok account showing a female fan tapping on the glass while players crash into it during a game, with the text, “So we’re calling this the boy aquarium now?”

The University of Cincinnati’s men’s ice hockey team took a different approach, filming themselves actually visiting a real aquarium. “POV: the boy aquarium visits the actual aquarium while on a team trip,” the overlay text reads.

But perhaps the biggest sign that the trend has gone mainstream came from "Heated Rivalry" star Hudson Williams himself. During a recent appearance on ‘The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon,Williams shared his amusement at the phenomenon.
“I’ve heard the term 'boy aquarium' … and they just sit at the glass and they’re like, ‘the boy aquarium!’” he said with a chuckle.
Whether you’re there for the romance novel fantasy or just looking for a group outing with lots of laughs, the boy aquarium delivers. Just maybe don’t tap on the glass.

title: The ‘Heated Rivalry’ Obsession and Why Fans Can’t (and Won’t) Quit It
summary: If "Heated Rivalry" is still on your mind, there may be a few reasons why. TODAY.com asked people with varying points of view to explain the "Heated Rivalry" obsession.
story: If this story has found you, chances are, “Heated Rivalry” is on your mind. It’s not in a way that’s fleeting either but, rather, all consuming. You’re still at the cottage. You haven’t stopped thinking about all the things she said, all the things she said. Your psyche keeps replaying its own version of a TikTok edit, a kaleidoscope of phone calls in Russian, shoes touching under tables, stupid Canadian wolf birds, room 1221, blueberry smoothies with extra banana and “you look pretty.”
If you know, you know. And, if viewership numbers are any indication, a whole lot of you know.

But for those who don’t, here’s the gist: “Heated Rivalry,” a steamy TV show that debuted in November about a romance between two rival hockey players, birthed a phenomenon so big and so fast, even executives at its U.S. streamer, HBO, are bewildered. It’s not just the show, either; its lead actors, Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie, have discovered the type of superstardom in two months that others in Hollywood take years and years to obtain.

So what is it about this show that’s made it so sensational? Why can’t we quit it? Why don’t we want to? These are among many questions we at TODAY.com have been tormented by. So, in search of answers, we reached out to a handful of people familiar with the material who could provide varying expertise and points of view. Here’s what they told us.
Meet the Voices

Jen Prokop is a romance critic who co-hosts the podcast “Fated Mates” alongside romance novelist Sarah MacLean.R.K. Russell is a former pro athlete who in 2019 became the first out active NFL player to identify as bisexual. He played for the Dallas Cowboys, Tampa Bay Buccaneers and Buffalo Bills and published a memoir, “The Yards Between Us,” in 2023.Dylan and Isabelle are two fans in their early 20s who lined up at 30 Rock in the wee hours for a chance to see Hudson Williams on “The Tonight Show.” Their last names are omitted to protect their privacy.Gayle Stever is a professor of psychology at Empire State University of New York who authored several books on media, celebrities and their fans, including 2018’s “The Psychology of Celebrity” and 2024’s “Parasocial Experiences.” She spoke to us via email.Kelly, aka K, host of the podcast “K, Let’s Talk” whose TikTok account has gone viral thanks to her videos with insightful analyses of “Heated Rivalry” scenes and characters. She’s just shy of 100,000 followers and has 81 videos “Heated Rivalry” playlist alone. Her last name is omitted to protect her privacy.
Jen Prokop is a romance critic who co-hosts the podcast “Fated Mates” alongside romance novelist Sarah MacLean.

R.K. Russell is a former pro athlete who in 2019 became the first out active NFL player to identify as bisexual. He played for the Dallas Cowboys, Tampa Bay Buccaneers and Buffalo Bills and published a memoir, “The Yards Between Us,” in 2023.

Dylan and Isabelle are two fans in their early 20s who lined up at 30 Rock in the wee hours for a chance to see Hudson Williams on “The Tonight Show.” Their last names are omitted to protect their privacy.

Gayle Stever is a professor of psychology at Empire State University of New York who authored several books on media, celebrities and their fans, including 2018’s “The Psychology of Celebrity” and 2024’s “Parasocial Experiences.” She spoke to us via email.

Kelly, aka K, host of the podcast “K, Let’s Talk” whose TikTok account has gone viral thanks to her videos with insightful analyses of “Heated Rivalry” scenes and characters. She’s just shy of 100,000 followers and has 81 videos “Heated Rivalry” playlist alone. Her last name is omitted to protect her privacy.
Questions and answers were lightly edited for length and clarity.
Let’s Discuss

Jen Prokop: “Romance is the reason. Romance is the most popular genre of books published. A romance TV show that really hits the ‘cinematic beats’ of a romance novel — I don’t think we’ve ever seen it. This feels to us in the romance genre what we get every time we read a romance novel. Welcome to the club. The thing about this adaptation is it never shies away from the idea that the feelings of Shane and Ilya are what’s really important. Romance readers are not ashamed of the idea that the people you fall in love with can change your whole life. When I see a romance adaptation that’s a little too cool for feelings … you have to really lean in to the idea that there is nothing shameful about love, and the people you fall in love with being important to your life and your happiness.”

Isabel: “People are looking for a TV show that depicts true romance. Women like to see nontoxic masculinity in a (romantic) relationship and you’re not forced to put yourself in that relationship. It’s not just straight women. I’m gay myself. You’re able to view it as a beautiful love story.”
Dylan: “I know there’s a big fanbase who are women, but for me, as a gay Asian man, I see myself in Shane. He wanted to depict queer joy. Seeing that on TV hit home for me.”

R.K. Russell: “It’s a story we don’t get to see and celebrate. Not just in TV but in real life. At this time there are no male professional athletes in the big five sports here that are out. That representation, and that taboo of male athletes being queer, vulnerable and soft, we don’t see and root for. ‘Heated Rivalry’ allowed for this subject that we have great intrigue of but little representation.”

Kelly: “I uniquely also played hockey, so I think that that’s another angle that I’m coming at the story from. Obviously it’s a love story — but at its core, I really identified with the pressure aspect of it. ... There’s a lot to unpack in terms of how, as a hockey player, and specifically a female hockey player, you’re expected to show up in those spaces. But even if you’ve never played hockey or (a sport) in general, I think everyone understands some version of that pressure or a deep desire to be seen or understood or loved for who you actually are and not a version of yourself that you create in order to conform to the environment.”

Gayle Stever: “I think that women are attracted to relationships that are between two social equals. The way society is structured currently, it is often the case that a relationship between a man and a woman has a power imbalance (not always but often). So when women enter a ‘fantasy’ scenario, part of the ideal is to remove that imbalance. Two men are social equals. ... One of the features (my friend who watches this tells me) is that the confidants for these men are woman. Women like being in this role.”

Russell: “It’s a representation that we want from men. You can be all the things as a man, and it doesn’t discredit you or make you less masculine. You can be a top athlete and performer in a physical presence and you can also, you know, be lovesick over a guy. Two things can exist. You can be both hard and soft. I think that’s really big. And I think we’re all looking for some love.”

Kelly: “I think for women, we identify with that feeling of otherness or exclusion that the two characters, even though they’re men, are experiencing. It draws a parallel to how women are supposed to conform to the world that we’re in. ... The other part of it: You do have to give credit to the fact that women love romance stories, and this is a really well crafted book, and the show itself is so intentionally made. ... That’s what a lot of people talk to me on my review videos about — the craft that went into the show feels different than a lot of the romance genre adaptations we’ve seen recently.”

Kelly: “The actors themselves, when they were doing a lot of the promotional run, are just so unfiltered and pushing up against that hyper-masculine world. It’s everywhere, but hockey in general, you don’t see that level of vulnerability or unmasking of what masculinity is traditionally associated with. I feel like that definitely resonates with everyone who feels constrained by expectations society places on them. It’s a very universal experience.”

Russell: “A lot of conversations that I’m having personally with queer athletes, both out and not out, is the the Scott Hunter and Kip story. It’s very close home for a lot of athletes. For most of us, we were athletes in the closet, having to date other people and explain our situation. Not wanting them to come into the closet, but to know that to interact with us is to be your half-self at times, especially publicly. That’s one example where the social conversation and the actual lived experience frayed at times, because a lot of people didn’t see the purpose of Episode 3 until they got to Episode 5. But for me, Episode 3 is really what made me lean in more to the show, because I saw myself in a lot of ways.”

Prokop: “‘50 Shades’ had a similar impact. ‘Bridgerton.’ As romance readers, we’re always kind of dumbfounded why people don’t look to us more for great stories, because clearly, like, we’ve got the juice. For many people, emotions and figuring out who you want to be with is a story that consumes people’s lives and young years. These are great stories. We can tell them without ruining them.”

Kelly: “A lot of people that I interact with online draw a lot of parallels to the show ‘Normal People.’ I don’t know if ‘Normal People’ had the same global impact, but the thing that people are drawing parallels to, at least on my end of social media, is the the very raw portrayal of isolation, feeling less-than, not meeting expectations. Those feelings are very much the same as how ‘Heated Rivalry’ is discussing that, even though ‘Heated Rivalry’ is a queer story. I think that for a lot of people that are around the age of these characters — because in both shows the characters are around the same age — that’s just a universal reality that everyone quietly deals with.”

title: Tim McGraw Announces 33-Date 'Pawn Shop Guitar' Summer Tour
summary: Tim McGraw announces his 2026 North American 'Pawn Shop Guitar' summer tour, including stadium shows with The Chicks, Lady A. Tickets available Feb 4-6.
story: Tim McGraw is picking up the band and heading back out onto the blacktop.

The country singer announced Feb. 3 that he’ll launch a sprawling 33-date North American summer tour in 2026, kicking things off July 9 in Bethel, New York, and wrapping Sept. 26 in West Palm Beach, Florida, after a cross-continental run.

Three stadium shows are planned — Fenway Park, Hersheypark Stadium and Target Field — with chart-toppers The Chicks and Lady A joining the lineup. As McGraw wrote in a press release, “I dare you to find more hits in one show!”
The 2026 run, called the “Pawn Shop Guitar” tour, precedes the release of McGraw’s new single, which shares the same name.

Fans eager to snag seats can jump into the McGrawONE+ presale beginning Feb. 4, with general tickets going on sale Feb. 6. Citi cardholders in the United States will have the opportunity to purchase presale tickets beginning Feb. 5, at 10 a.m. local time, with with access continuing through 10 p.m. the same day via the Citi Entertainment program.

After canceling the remainder of his 2024 “Standing Room Only” tour to focus on his health, McGraw spent much of the past year recovering from a series of procedures , including multiple back surgeries and double knee replacements that forced him off the road.
McGraw has been candid about his recovery, telling the Bobby Bones Show he was on “the upswing.”
“Some mornings are good, some mornings take a while, but it’s getting there,” he said.

title: ‘The Kelly Clarkson Show’ to End After 7 Seasons: ‘This Was Not an Easy Decision’
summary: Kelly Clarkson has announced the current season of her daytime talk show will be its last.
story: Kelly Clarkson has announced the current season of her daytime talk show will be its last.
On Feb. 2, the singer and host posted a statement on her Instagram confirming that “The Kelly Clarkson Show” will end after seven seasons.

“I have been extremely fortunate to work with such an outstanding group of people at (‘The Kelly Clarkson Show’), both in Los Angeles and New York,” she began. “There have been so many amazing moments and shows over these seven seasons. I am forever grateful and honored to have worked alongside the greatest band and crew you could hope for, all the talent and inspiring people who have shared their time and lives with us, all the fans who have supported our show, and to NBC for always being such a supportive and incredible partner.”
She went on to say that the crew she has worked with makes it difficult for her to move on from the show.

“Because of all that, this was not an easy decision — but this season will be my last hosting TKCS,” she continued. “Stepping away from the daily schedule will allow me to prioritize my kids, which feels necessary and right for this next chapter of our lives.”

Clarkson shared 11-year-old daughter River Rose and 9-year-old son Remy with her late ex-husband Brandon Blackson. In August 2025, Blackson’s family shared in a statement that he died at the age of 48 following a three-year battle with cancer.
Although “The Kelly Clarkson Show” is concluding, Clarkson wanted her fans to know that she isn’t saying goodbye.
“I’ll still be making music, playing shows here and there, and you may catch me on ‘The Voice' from time to time’…you never know where I might show up next,” she assured them.

At the end of her statement, she added, “But for now, I want to thank y’all so much for allowing our show to be a part of your lives, and for believing in us and hanging with us for seven incredible years.”

New episodes of Season 7 will continue to air through fall 2026, the show confirmed in a press release. The show’s staff and crew were informed about the talk show ending the same day Clarkson shared the news with her viewers.

“The Kelly Clarkson Show” arrived in 2019 and allowed the Grammy winner to show off her interviewing skills and her impressive vocals at the same time, with her interviews and her “Kellyoke” performances going viral. The show earned 24 Daytime Emmy Awards during its run.

title: Ludacris Says Filming a Hot Sauce Commercial Is ‘Quite Similar’ to Filming a Music Video
summary: Ahead of the Super Bowl, Ludacris says filming his latest commercial for Frank's RedHot (alongside a goat) reminded him of filming music videos.
story: Ludacris knows his way around a commercial set.

On Feb, 2 Frank's RedHot released an ad starring the three-time Grammy winner, actor and provider of the soundtrack to many millennials’ high school experiences. In the spot titled, “Eat The GOAT,” the “Roll Out” rapper festoons his home with bottle after bottle of Frank’s RedHot, putting, as their tagline states, “that s--- on everything.”
Not only that, but he stars alongside a real, live, bleating, rapping goat.
“It almost felt like I was shooting a music video,” Ludacris tells TODAY.com, adding that he’s been a regular Frank’s RedHot user for “decades.”

“I’m a healthy eater but I still love my hot sauce,” he says, adding that the ad flips the sports acronym for “greatest of all time” into “something fun and delicious that fans can actually do.” In other words, rather than be the GOAT, fans can eat the GOAT.

In the chuckle-worthy commercial, Ludacris says you “can’t forget the GOAT” while he throws the ultimate Super Bowl party. In a cameo, Chingy pops in to ask, “That homie right thurr?” in a nod to his 2003 hit “Right Thurr.”

“I was able to get some of my artists together that were on ‘Disturbing tha Peace’ and so it felt like a family reunion,” he says, shouting out cameos from collaborators Shawnna, Chingy and I-20. “It was really dope in terms of how they had the setup, and us being involved in the creative aspect of it.”
At the end of the commercial, we see an actual goat, sporting sunglasses, who turns its head, raps, much to everyone’s surprise, then bleats, as goats are wont to do.

While Ludacris holds music in his “Number One Spot” — which actually soundtracks the hot sauce commercial — he says, when he films ads like his iconic rap-themed one for peanut butter brand Jif, he is reminded of making music videos.

“I would say, depending on the commercial, it’s actually quite similar, because when you think about it, even though a commercial can be 20, 30, seconds long, even 60 seconds, a music video usually is around two to three minutes; they feel the same,” he says.

It should be pointed out that Ludacris has a whopping 32 commercial credits to his name, including ads for State Farm and a past Super Bowl commerical for Haagen Dazs with fellow “Fast & Furious” stars Michelle Rodriguez and Vin Diesel.

“When you’re shooting a commercial for a certain brand, they take a very long time making sure that everything is perfect, whereas in music videos, you can have setups and you can kind of do things a little quicker,” he says.
“All that being said, they feel similar, because the same amount of time you do to shoot a commercial is almost the same amount of time it is to shoot a music video.”
You can watch the full ad on Frank’s RedHot’s YouTube channel.

title: Keke Palmer Shares Her Dating Hot Take: ‘I Never Want To Live Together’
summary: Keke Palmer values time by herself
story: Keke Palmer values time by herself.
The Emmy winner shared what may be an unpopular opinion about her romantic life when she appeared on TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle on Feb. 2.
Sheinelle Jones and Jenna Bush Hager brought up the “hot take dating” trend, in which people dive right in and discuss potentially sensitive topics on a first date.

“I think that’s actually good,” Palmer, 32, said. “Yeah, just get it out of the way because sometimes you end up talking to somebody, y’all never end up talking about anything real, any of the things that actually show compatibility likewise.”
Jenna and Palmer then played out what a first date might look like when taking part in the trend.
“One thing I need you to know is I never want to live together,” Palmer said, stunning Sheinelle and Jenna.
“Should I take it personally?” Jenna asked, while she, Sheinelle and Palmer laughed.
“I like my alone time,” the actor explained.
When Jenna asked if her stance was genuine, Palmer, who has a 2-year-old son, replied, “That’s real.”
“I’m speaking as the Keke,” she continued.

“Honestly, I just feel like, Whoopi Goldberg said it best once. She said, ‘I don’t want anybody in my house.’ And I just feel that,” she said, alluding to a quote from the comedian's 2016 New York Times interview.

“I’m much happier on my own. I can spend as much time with somebody as I want to spend, but I’m not looking to be with somebody forever or live with someone,” Goldberg said at the time. “I don’t want somebody in my house.”
Sheinelle was stunned that the "Nope" star wasn’t joking.
“I’m serious. Married, even,” Palmer said about how far she’d be willing to go with living alone.
When Sheinelle suggested the spouse live “around the corner,” Palmer was all in.
“Around the corner would be great,” she said.
“What about a guest house?” Jenna chimed in.
“He can be in a guest house, you know what I’m saying?” Palmer said. “We can be on the same land, but I’m over there and he’s over there. At best separate rooms.”
She also shot down Sheinelle talking about the romance of having two toothbrushes side-by-side in a bathroom.
“Uh-uh. No. So he can mess my bathroom?” Palmer replied.
“But think about how fun it would be to be like, ‘I’m going over to my man’s house,” she added. “It keeps it fun and exciting. I want to go sit on his couch."
“And he better clean when you’re coming over,” Jenna said.
“That’s what I’m saying,” Palmer said.

title: The Best Dressed Stars of the 2026 Grammys, According to Fashion Experts
summary: These are the standout looks from the 2026 and the best dressed moments according to fashion exports Hayley Paige, June Ambrose, Jenna Lyons and Zanna Roberts Rossi.
story: Awards season is always full of showstopping style moments, but the Grammy Awards is always a standout occasion since celebrities tend to take a few more fashion risks.
If you watched the 2026 Grammy Awards on Feb. 1, you saw that many of those risks paid off. They also created some memorable red carpet moments that fashion lovers won't soon be forgetting.

“The night’s strongest looks weren’t chasing trends, they were composing, building rhythm through proportion, using fabric like instrumentation, and hiding easter eggs only true fans (and fashion nerds) would catch," designer Hayley Paige told TODAY.com.

Fashion experts June Ambrose, Jenna Lyons and Zanna Roberts Rossi are also obsessed with the mix of edgy and demure looks that hit the Grammys red carpet, and they joined "TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle" to discuss the night's best styles on Feb. 2.
Below, we're sharing the top looks of the night and commentary from Paige, Ambrose, Lyons and Roberts Rossi.
Olivia Dean
Ambrose, Lyons and Roberts Rossi all agreed on "TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle" that Olivia Dean was the best-dressed woman on the Grammys red carpet in her black and white Chanel gown.
Meanwhile, Paige described her look as "sartorial romance, feathered and flawless."

"This is colorblocking as poetry. The interplay of texture, tone and movement felt flirty yet immaculately controlled. Also very Chanel in spirit, but modern in execution. The feathers added softness without chaos. The tailoring kept everything disciplined," she said.
Addison Rae

Addison Rae had her own Marilyn Monroe moment in a white gown featuring a plunging neckline. The singer's voluminous skirt had a wide silhouette that tapered off into ruffles in the front. In the back, the skirt's volume was even more pronounced and far shorter.
Lyons, Ambrose and Roberts Rossi appreciated the mix of elegance and playfulness.
Teyana Taylor

Teyana Taylor came dressed to impress in a bejeweled gown that left little to the imagination. The singer and actor, who recently picked up a Golden Globe for "One Battle After Another," showed off some skin in the curve-hugging gown that featured a subtle, sparkly train.
"She is not playing fair," Ambrose said, while complimenting Taylor's fit physique.
"She literally brought kink to the carpet," Ambrose added.
Sabrina Carpenter
Sabrina Carpenter opted for an elegant look at the Grammys in a bejeweled Valentino gown.

“This look understood seduction through editing. The silhouette is classic, almost demure, but the surface tells another story: shimmer applied with intention, not excess. There’s a pin-up whisper here, but it’s refined, not costumey,” Paige said.
Miley Cyrus
Ambrose, Lyons and Roberts Rossi said it was easy to award Miley Cyrus the title of "Best Accessories" for the night.
"It was very rock star chic," Lyons said.

The singer looked cool in a black leather jacket, white blouse and black pants, but it was her massive gold brooch that really made heads turn. Cyrus upped the cool factor with black leather gloves and sunglasses.
Doechii
Representing the Best Glam category, Doechii caught the attention of Roberts Rossi, Lyons and Ambrose.
“It was a total 180 for her,” Roberts Rossi said, adding that she appreciated the “boho glam” ensemble and beauty look.

The musician looked stunning in a gown that featured a burnt orange bust with crisscross detailing and an impressive embellished purple train. The high-low silhouette showed off the singer's legs in the front and gave fans a glimpse at her strappy sandals.
Sombr
Roberts Rossi, Lyons and Ambrose agree that Sombr earned the title of best-dressed man at the 2026 Grammys.
"He hit every note. He's unbelievably chic," Lyons said.
Paige was also impressed with the singer's psychedelic Valentino ensemble.
"It’s giving rock god glam with a disco heartbeat," she said.
Sombr brought confidence to the red carpet in the sequined suit and lacy blouse that featured a cutout in the middle, revealing his chest.
"This felt like a modern echo of Mick Jagger’s most dangerous years: tactile, theatrical and just loose enough to feel alive. The shine wasn’t precious; it was defiant yet effortless," Paige said.
EJAE, Audrey Nuna and Rei Ami
